title: Latest News from Science Magazine
description: Keyword search result for Alerts: News Weekly Headlines -- New results matching your keyword search query (updated daily).

items.title: In unreleased document, fired U.S. science board issues stark warning about keeping pace with China
items.description: The National Science Board urges robust response to China’s rise in cover letter to its biennial report on the global research enterprise

items.title: Did this scientist go too far trying to save Ecuador’s wildlife?
items.description: Alejandro Arteaga’s efforts to identify and protect tropical reptiles and amphibians have entangled him in controversy

items.title: Molecules emerge as a new kind of building block for quantum computers
items.description: Storing quantum information in designer molecules could hold advantages over atoms, ions, and other kinds of qubits

items.title: Boy’s brain tumor tied to gene therapy
items.description: Researchers report first cancer caused by virus widely used to deliver genes, but say risk is low

items.title: Gotcha! Odd language mistakes may help identify fake papers
items.description: A small investigation found identical errors and phrases across more than 200 papers, suggesting they were produced by paper mills

items.title: Ghost of long-extinct ancestor lives on in people today
items.description: Ancient Homo erectus teeth from China yield protein variants seen in Denisovans—and us

items.title: Fluffy ice could imperil spacecraft landings on ocean moons
items.description: Water erupted from cryovolcanoes may freeze into thick but fragile layers on Europa and Enceladus

items.title: Virologist accused of starting COVID-19 will fight U.S. ban on funding
items.description: HHS memo says Ralph Baric had a “pattern of deception” in describing virus studies long before pandemic

items.title: Most dementia patients have multiple brain diseases. How should they be treated?
items.description: Growing awareness of “copathology” inspires new diagnostic tests and clinical trials

items.title: Chinese postdocs in U.S. hit with a wave of prosecutions and deportations
items.description: The U.S. government has recently convicted multiple postdocs for improper shipments of biological materials. Some see a replay of the 2018 China Initiative
